issues.title: Verify Supabase + Vercel free-tier facts and document baseline DB size
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Verify current Supabase and Vercel free-tier limits against live documentation and measure the existing local database size to confirm the migration fits within free-tier constraints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Supabase free-tier limits documented: storage (MB), MAU, concurrent connections, backup retention, project pause behavior, restore procedure
- [ ] Vercel hobby-tier limits documented: egress, build minutes, serverless function limits, preview deploy constraints
- [ ] Current local Postgres database size measured (`pg_database_size`) and recorded
- [ ] Table-by-table size breakdown recorded (identify largest tables)
- [ ] Confirmation that current data fits within 500 MB Supabase free tier (with headroom estimate)
- [ ] Findings written to `docs/design-hosting/baseline-facts.md`

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §8 (Cost Profile) — ⚠️ markers requiring user verification
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.3 (Database) — free-tier assumptions
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/04-deployment-cicd.md` §1 (Hosting Options Comparison)

## Dependencies
None — this is the first issue and unblocks all subsequent work.

## Out of Scope
- Actually creating Supabase/Vercel accounts (that's TJ-004)
- Schema changes or migrations
- Custom domain decisions (tracked separately in design.md §17)

issues.title: Create local Supabase dev stack and sanitized seed data runbook
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Set up a local Supabase development environment (`supabase init` + `supabase start`) with sanitized seed data so all team members can develop and test RLS policies, auth flows, and migrations locally without touching remote Supabase projects.

This also fulfills Redfoot's pre-Phase 1 residual ask: a concrete local/dev repro runbook.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `supabase/` directory initialized in repo root with `config.toml`
- [ ] `supabase start` brings up local Postgres, Auth, and Studio
- [ ] Sanitized seed SQL file created (`supabase/seed.sql`) with realistic but anonymized financial data
- [ ] Runbook written at `docs/design-hosting/local-dev-runbook.md` with step-by-step commands
- [ ] Runbook covers: install Supabase CLI, start local stack, run seed, run migrations, run tests, stop/reset
- [ ] At least one team member (not the author) can follow the runbook cold and get a working local env

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/reviews/redfoot-rereview.md` — residual ask for local/dev repro runbook
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 (Test Strategy) — Phase 0/1 test requirements
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 (Migration Plan) Phase 0

## Dependencies
- TJ-000 (need baseline DB facts to size seed data appropriately)

## Out of Scope
- Remote Supabase project provisioning (TJ-004)
- RLS policy implementation (TJ-009)
- Production seed data or real financial records

issues.title: Create secrets management plan and env var inventory
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Document every environment variable the hosted stack will need, where each secret lives (GitHub Actions secrets, Vercel dashboard, local `.env`), and the public/private split. Create a template `.env.example` for local development.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Env var inventory table created in `docs/design-hosting/env-vars.md` listing: variable name, exposure (public/server-only), used by (frontend/backend/CI), source (Vercel/GitHub/local)
- [ ] Covers all vars from design.md §10: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `SUPABASE_DB_DIRECT_URL`, `SUPABASE_DB_POOL_URL`, `DATABASE_URL`
- [ ] `.env.example` file created at repo root with placeholder values and comments
- [ ] Documented which secrets go in GitHub Actions protected secrets vs Vercel env vars
- [ ] CI guard rule documented: `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` must never appear in `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` variables
- [ ] No real secrets committed — only placeholders and documentation

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §10 (Environment, Secrets & Connection Pooling)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/02-frontend-strategy.md` — env var layout table
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/04-deployment-cicd.md` §3 (Secrets Management)

## Dependencies
- TJ-000 (need confirmed Supabase project structure to enumerate vars)

## Out of Scope
- Actually setting secrets in Vercel/GitHub (that happens in TJ-004 and TJ-019)
- Secret rotation procedures (future work)

issues.title: Classify table ownership: household vs owner-private vs global reference
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Produce a definitive classification of every existing database table into one of three ownership categories: household-scoped (gets `household_id`), owner-private (gets `owner_user_id`), or global reference (readable by all authenticated users). This classification drives the Alembic migration in TJ-005.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every table in `apps/backend/app/schema/*.py` classified with rationale
- [ ] Classification matches Section 06 survey table and design.md §4.3/§6
- [ ] Output is a Markdown table in `docs/design-hosting/table-classification.md`
- [ ] Columns: table name, current model file, ownership category, gets `household_id` (Y/N), gets `owner_user_id` (Y/N), notes
- [ ] Edge cases documented: `note` (owner-private), `backtestrun` (owner-private, optionally promotable), `trading_account_config` (split: metadata=household, secrets=owner-private)
- [ ] Reviewed against McManus's Section 06 existing schema survey

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/06-data-architecture.md` — Existing schema survey table
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.3 (Database) and §6 (Data Architecture)
- See `apps/backend/app/schema/` — current SQLModel definitions

## Dependencies
- TJ-000 (baseline DB size informs whether any tables should be excluded from migration)

## Out of Scope
- Actually writing the Alembic migration (TJ-005)
- raw/compute/cooked schema creation (TJ-006)
- RLS policy design (TJ-009)

issues.title: Provision Supabase projects for dev, preview, and production
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create three separate Supabase projects (local-dev, preview, production) under Jony's `cohenjo` org to ensure environment isolation. Configure Google OAuth provider on each. Record project refs and connection URLs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Three Supabase projects created with distinct project refs
- [ ] Google OAuth enabled on each project (Client ID/Secret from Google Cloud Console)
- [ ] Redirect URLs configured per design: localhost, preview, and production callback paths
- [ ] Project refs, anon keys, and connection URLs recorded in secrets vault (not committed)
- [ ] CI assertion documented: preview project ref ≠ production project ref
- [ ] Direct and pooled connection URLs verified and documented per design.md §10
- [ ] Local Supabase CLI linked to dev project for migrations

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 1 step 1 — separate projects for local/dev, preview, production
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §10 (Environment, Secrets & Connection Pooling)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/03-auth-sharing-security.md` §2.1 (Supabase project configuration)

## Dependencies
- TJ-000 (verified free-tier limits)
- TJ-002 (env var inventory tells us which secrets to capture)

## Out of Scope
- Schema migration (TJ-005)
- Vercel project setup (TJ-019)
- Custom domain decisions (deferred per design.md §17)

issues.title: Add household_id, owner_user_id, and audit columns via Alembic migration
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Write Alembic migrations that add `household_id`, `owner_user_id`, `created_by`, `updated_by`, and `deleted_at` columns to all existing tables based on TJ-003's classification. Create the core household and household_members tables. Validate financial totals match before and after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `households` table created per design.md §5 DDL (with `deleted_at`)
- [ ] `household_role` enum created (`owner`, `member`, `viewer`)
- [ ] `household_members` table created per design.md §5 DDL (with `left_at`, `invited_by`)
- [ ] `household_invites` table created per design.md §5 DDL
- [ ] All household-scoped tables get `household_id uuid` column (nullable initially for backfill)
- [ ] All owner-private tables get `owner_user_id uuid` column
- [ ] Audit columns added: `created_by`, `updated_by`, `deleted_at` where missing
- [ ] `numeric(18, 6)` verified for all monetary columns per design.md §4.3
- [ ] Alembic upgrade/downgrade tested locally with row-count and financial-total parity
- [ ] Downgrade documented with data-loss expectations

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (Auth & Sharing) — household DDL
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 0 steps 3-5
- See `docs/design-hosting/table-classification.md` (output of TJ-003)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/06-data-architecture.md` — schema survey

## Dependencies
- TJ-003 (table classification drives which columns go where)
- TJ-004 (need Supabase dev project to test migration against)

## Out of Scope
- Backfilling actual household data (TJ-013)
- RLS policies (TJ-009)
- NOT NULL constraints on household_id (added after backfill in TJ-013)

issues.title: Create raw/compute/cooked schema layering and initial tables
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create the three-layer schema structure (`raw`, `compute`, `cooked`) in Supabase Postgres with initial tables for trade data, P&L summaries, and position snapshots. Create the `compute_runs` job-status table and `household_refresh_state` table.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `raw`, `compute`, `cooked` schemas created via Alembic migration
- [ ] Initial raw tables: `raw_trades_import`, `raw_market_data` (at minimum)
- [ ] Initial compute tables: `compute_pnl_daily`, `compute_trade_matches`
- [ ] Initial cooked tables: `cooked_pnl_summary`, `cooked_position_snapshot`
- [ ] `compute_runs` table created with columns: `id`, `household_id`, `domain`, `status` (queued/running/succeeded/failed), `started_at`, `finished_at`, `source_run_id`, `row_counts`, `reconciliation_totals`, `error_summary`
- [ ] `household_refresh_state` table created per design.md §6/§14
- [ ] All tables have `household_id` for scoping
- [ ] All monetary columns use `numeric(18, 6)`
- [ ] Migration tested locally against Supabase dev project

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §6 (Data Architecture) — raw/compute/cooked layering
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/06-data-architecture.md` — full data architecture
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §14 (Observability) — compute_runs and household_refresh_state

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (need Supabase dev project)
- TJ-005 (household tables must exist before FK references)

## Out of Scope
- Compute worker implementation (TJ-012)
- Cooked-table reconciliation logic (TJ-014)
- RLS on raw/compute schemas (workers use service-role; only cooked needs user-facing RLS)

issues.title: Implement RLS helper functions and core policies
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Implement the canonical RLS helper functions (`household_role_for`, `is_household_member`, `can_write_household`, `is_household_owner`) and apply RLS policies to all household-scoped and owner-private tables. This is the security foundation — a bad policy can leak financial data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four RLS helper functions created per design.md §5 exact DDL
- [ ] `REVOKE ALL ... FROM public` + `GRANT EXECUTE ... TO authenticated` applied
- [ ] RLS enabled and forced on all household-scoped tables
- [ ] Select/Insert/Update policies applied per design.md §5 pattern (trades example)
- [ ] Owner-private tables (notes, backtests) have `owner_user_id = auth.uid()` policies
- [ ] Global reference tables (market data, tickers) have `SELECT` for authenticated, write only for service role
- [ ] Triggers/column grants prevent mutation of `household_id`, `created_by`, audit fields after insert
- [ ] RLS integration test suite proving: anonymous denied, non-member denied, viewer write denied, member write allowed, owner admin allowed, ex-member denied
- [ ] Soft-deleted household member (`left_at IS NOT NULL`) cannot access data

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (RLS pattern) — canonical helper functions and policy DDL
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 1 — required RLS test proofs
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/03-auth-sharing-security.md` — full security design

## Dependencies
- TJ-005 (household tables and columns must exist)

## Out of Scope
- Household invite flow UX (TJ-021)
- Sharing-specific RLS refinements (TJ-022)
- Application-level auth middleware (TJ-016)

issues.title: Set up CI/CD scaffolding: PR validation + Vercel git integration
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create GitHub Actions workflows for PR validation (frontend lint/typecheck/test, backend lint/typecheck/test, Docker build validation) and configure the main-merge pipeline for Vercel auto-deploy and Alembic migration execution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/pr-validation.yml` — runs on PR: frontend lint+typecheck+vitest, backend lint+typecheck+pytest, Docker build smoke
- [ ] `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — runs on main merge: triggers Alembic `upgrade head` against Supabase dev/preview
- [ ] CI assertion: preview Supabase project ref ≠ production project ref (fail if equal)
- [ ] Secrets configured in GitHub Actions: `SUPABASE_DB_DIRECT_URL`, `SUPABASE_DB_POOL_URL` (for preview/dev only initially)
- [ ] Vercel project connected to GitHub repo with auto-deploy on push to main
- [ ] Preview deploys working on PRs via Vercel

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.5 (CI/CD & Deployment)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/04-deployment-cicd.md` §2 (Deployment Topology)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 1 step 1 — CI fails if preview targets production

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (need Supabase projects provisioned for CI secrets)

## Out of Scope
- Nightly cron jobs (TJ-029)
- Production deploy pipeline (TJ-027)
- Backend container push to GHCR (future work)

issues.title: Set up encrypted pg_dump backup cron and restore rehearsal
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Implement an encrypted local `pg_dump` offload cron from Phase 1, as specified in the design, since Supabase free-tier backup guarantees are plan-dependent. Rehearse restoring the dump into local Docker Postgres.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script created: `scripts/backup-supabase.sh` — runs `pg_dump` against Supabase direct URL, encrypts output with GPG/age
- [ ] Script is idempotent and safe to run from cron or manually
- [ ] Backup files stored locally (gitignored) with date-stamped filenames
- [ ] Restore script created: `scripts/restore-backup.sh` — decrypts and loads into local Docker Postgres
- [ ] Restore rehearsal documented and verified: row counts and financial totals match source
- [ ] Backup process documented in `docs/design-hosting/local-dev-runbook.md`
- [ ] Never commits dump files to git

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §6 (Backups) — encrypted pg_dump offload cron
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §12 (Rollback Rehearsal) Phase 1 — restore procedure
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §16 Risk #2 — Supabase free-tier backup mitigation

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (need Supabase project with connection URL)
- TJ-005 (need schema migrated to have meaningful backup)

## Out of Scope
- Automated nightly cron scheduling (TJ-029)
- Supabase managed backup configuration (plan-dependent)

issues.title: Wire manual trade entry flows to work against Supabase schema
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Update the existing FastAPI manual trade entry endpoints to work against the new Supabase schema with `household_id` columns and Supabase JWT validation. This is the first proof that the hosted schema works for real CRUD operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manual trade CRUD endpoints (`ManualTrade`) work against Supabase Postgres
- [ ] FastAPI validates Supabase JWT via JWKS endpoint (not local python-jose minting)
- [ ] `household_id` correctly populated on new trade entries
- [ ] Connection uses pooled URL for web traffic with `pool_pre_ping=True` and `statement_cache_size=0`
- [ ] Direct URL used for batch operations
- [ ] RLS policies enforced — trades only visible to household members
- [ ] Existing pytest tests updated and passing

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.4 (Backend / Heavy Compute)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §10 (Connection Pooling)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/05-backend-strategy.md` — endpoint inventory

## Dependencies
- TJ-005 (schema with household columns)
- TJ-007 (RLS policies in place)

## Out of Scope
- Migrating to Server Actions (TJ-018)
- File upload endpoints
- Broker sync endpoints (IBKR out of scope)

issues.title: Implement compute worker framework with raw → compute → cooked pipeline
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Build the local Docker worker framework that reads raw tables, runs compute jobs, and publishes to cooked tables. Workers claim jobs from `compute_runs`, write intermediates to compute tables, and only publish cooked data on successful reconciliation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worker framework in `apps/backend/` that polls/claims `compute_runs` rows with `status='queued'`
- [ ] Job lifecycle: `queued` → `running` → `succeeded`/`failed` with timestamps
- [ ] At least one complete pipeline: raw trades → `compute_pnl_daily` → `cooked_pnl_summary`
- [ ] Publish-only-on-success: cooked rows written only after reconciliation SQL passes
- [ ] Reconciliation checks: raw → compute → cooked totals match for P&L using exact `numeric`/`Decimal`
- [ ] `household_refresh_state` updated on success/failure
- [ ] Worker connects to Supabase via TLS using direct Postgres credentials
- [ ] Idempotent: re-running a succeeded job produces same cooked output

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §6 (Data Architecture) — refresh strategy, cooked correctness
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.4 (Backend / Heavy Compute) — worker description
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §14 (Observability) — compute_runs tracking

## Dependencies
- TJ-006 (raw/compute/cooked tables must exist)
- TJ-010 (trade data must be writable to Supabase)

## Out of Scope
- Docker healthcheck/restart config (TJ-028)
- GitHub Actions cron scheduling (TJ-029)
- UI staleness indicators (TJ-020)

issues.title: Backfill existing data from local Postgres to Supabase
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Migrate existing data from local Postgres to Supabase: load sanitized snapshot, create Jony's personal household, backfill `household_id` on all rows, and add NOT NULL constraints after backfill. Validate financial totals match.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration script loads sanitized snapshot from local Postgres into Supabase
- [ ] Personal household created for Jony with `owner` role in `household_members`
- [ ] All household-scoped rows backfilled with Jony's `household_id`
- [ ] All owner-private rows backfilled with Jony's `owner_user_id`
- [ ] NOT NULL constraints added to `household_id` and `owner_user_id` after backfill
- [ ] Row counts verified: source vs destination for every table
- [ ] Financial totals verified: trades, matched trades, daily summaries, finance snapshots, plans, insurance, pensions, positions
- [ ] Backfill script is idempotent (safe to re-run)

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.3 — backfill migration description
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 1 steps 3, 7 — snapshot load and backfill
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 0 — row-count and financial-total parity

## Dependencies
- TJ-005 (household columns exist)
- TJ-006 (raw/compute/cooked schemas exist)
- TJ-007 (RLS policies exist to validate access after backfill)

## Out of Scope
- Market data migration (global reference data loaded separately)
- Compute pipeline (TJ-011 handles raw → cooked refresh)
- Production data migration (this is dev/preview; prod happens in TJ-027)

issues.title: Cooked-table reconciliation tests and data integrity validation
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create the test suite that validates cooked-table correctness: raw → compute → cooked reconciliation using exact numeric/Decimal expectations. This is the quality gate that prevents incorrect financial data from reaching the UI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pytest suite comparing raw → compute → cooked totals for: P&L, positions, planning dashboards
- [ ] Tests use exact `numeric`/`Decimal` expectations (no float comparison)
- [ ] Tests run against local Supabase dev project with seed data
- [ ] Test for: successful compute run produces correct cooked data
- [ ] Test for: failed reconciliation prevents cooked publish (old snapshot remains)
- [ ] Test for: `household_refresh_state` correctly reflects fresh/stale/failed states
- [ ] Integration with compute worker (TJ-011) — tests trigger a compute run and validate output

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §6 (Cooked correctness) — reconciliation requirements
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 1 — sanitized fixture with approved before/after totals
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §14 (Observability) — compute_runs status tracking

## Dependencies
- TJ-011 (compute worker must exist to test)
- TJ-012 (backfilled data needed as test baseline)

## Out of Scope
- UI display of staleness indicators (TJ-020)
- Worker retry/backoff testing (TJ-028)
- Performance testing of compute pipeline

issues.title: Migrate hardcoded env values and API URL references to env vars
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Replace all hardcoded database credentials, API URLs, and configuration values with environment variable references per the env var inventory from TJ-002. This is a prerequisite for multi-environment deployment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All hardcoded `DATABASE_URL` values in `docker-compose.yml` and backend config replaced with `${DATABASE_URL}` references
- [ ] All hardcoded CORS `allow_origins=["*"]` replaced with environment-driven origin list
- [ ] Frontend `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` references audited — no hardcoded localhost URLs in committed code
- [ ] Backend connection strings support both direct and pooled URLs per design.md §10
- [ ] `.env.example` updated with all new variables
- [ ] No real secrets in committed files (verify with `git grep` for common secret patterns)
- [ ] Existing tests still pass after env var migration

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/env-vars.md` (output of TJ-002)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §10 (Environment, Secrets & Connection Pooling)

## Dependencies
- TJ-002 (env var inventory must exist)

## Out of Scope
- Vercel env var configuration (TJ-019)
- GitHub Actions secrets (TJ-008)

## File paths to check
- `docker-compose.yml`
- `apps/backend/main.py` (CORS config)
- `apps/backend/app/database.py` or equivalent DB config
- `apps/frontend/next.config.ts` (API rewrite rules)
- `apps/frontend/src/` — grep for hardcoded API URLs

issues.title: Add @supabase/ssr middleware and Supabase client helpers
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Add `@supabase/ssr` package and create the foundational Supabase client infrastructure: browser client, server client, middleware for session refresh. This is boilerplate but must be correct — it's the auth backbone.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `@supabase/ssr` and `@supabase/supabase-js` added to `apps/frontend/package.json`
- [ ] `apps/frontend/src/lib/supabase/browser.ts` — `createBrowserClient` helper
- [ ] `apps/frontend/src/lib/supabase/server.ts` — `createServerClient` helper using cookies
- [ ] `apps/frontend/src/middleware.ts` — refreshes session per request, Edge runtime
- [ ] Middleware redirects unauthenticated users to `/signin` for protected routes
- [ ] Public routes (landing, signin, auth callback) are excluded from auth check
- [ ] Environment variables used: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
- [ ] No `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` imported in any client-side code

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.1 (Frontend) — SSR client split, middleware
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/02-frontend-strategy.md` — env var layout, middleware details

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (need Supabase project URL and anon key)

## Out of Scope
- Google OAuth flow implementation (TJ-016)
- Server Action CRUD (TJ-018)
- Service-role client usage

## File paths
- `apps/frontend/package.json`
- `apps/frontend/src/lib/supabase/browser.ts` (create)
- `apps/frontend/src/lib/supabase/server.ts` (create)
- `apps/frontend/src/middleware.ts` (create)

issues.title: Implement Google OAuth sign-in flow with Supabase Auth
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Build the sign-in, auth callback, and sign-out flows using Supabase Auth with Google OAuth. Cookies must be `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Lax`. No tokens in localStorage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/signin` page with Google sign-in button
- [ ] `/auth/callback` route handler that exchanges code for session
- [ ] Sign-out flow that clears session and redirects to `/signin`
- [ ] Cookies set with `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Lax`
- [ ] No tokens stored in `localStorage`
- [ ] Session refresh working via middleware (from TJ-015)
- [ ] Preview callback URL strategy tested per design.md §4.1
- [ ] Auth-protected pages use `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` — no public ISR for household data
- [ ] Vitest tests for middleware path classification and safe redirect validation

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.2 (Auth & Security)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (Auth & Sharing) — full auth UX flow
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/03-auth-sharing-security.md` §2 (Recommended setup)
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/02-frontend-strategy.md` — preview deploy OAuth strategy

## Dependencies
- TJ-015 (Supabase client infrastructure)
- TJ-004 (Google OAuth configured in Supabase dashboard)

## Out of Scope
- Household invite accept flow (TJ-021)
- Email magic link (deferred per design.md §17 — Google only initially)
- MFA (future work, plan-dependent)

issues.title: Replace python-jose JWT validation with Supabase JWKS verification
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Replace the existing `python-jose` / `passlib` JWT flow in FastAPI with Supabase JWKS verification. FastAPI stops minting its own tokens and instead validates Supabase-issued JWTs by fetching the JWKS endpoint. This is a transitional step while FastAPI endpoints still exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `python-jose` and `passlib` JWT minting code removed or disabled
- [ ] New auth dependency that fetches Supabase JWKS and validates: signature, issuer, audience, expiry, UUID `sub`
- [ ] Auth dependency injected into all remaining FastAPI endpoints
- [ ] JWKS endpoint URL derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
- [ ] JWKS response cached with appropriate TTL (e.g., 1 hour)
- [ ] Tests verify: valid Supabase JWT accepted, expired JWT rejected, wrong issuer rejected, missing token rejected
- [ ] No FastAPI endpoints mint new JWTs

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.2 — FastAPI verifies Supabase JWTs via JWKS
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (RLS pattern) — transitional endpoint validation
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/03-auth-sharing-security.md` §2

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (need Supabase project for JWKS endpoint)
- TJ-010 (FastAPI endpoints must be working against Supabase)

## Out of Scope
- Removing FastAPI endpoints entirely (TJ-018)
- Frontend auth implementation (TJ-016)

## File paths
- `apps/backend/app/api/auth.py` or equivalent auth module
- `apps/backend/app/dependencies/` — auth dependency
- `apps/backend/requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml` — remove python-jose if no longer needed

issues.title: Migrate CRUD endpoints from FastAPI to Next.js Server Actions
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Move ~20 CRUD endpoints from FastAPI to Next.js Server Actions per design.md §4.4. Server Actions use user-scoped Supabase SSR clients so RLS enforces access. Start with read-only dashboard endpoints, then move simple CRUD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read-only dashboard endpoints moved to Server Actions first (cooked table reads)
- [ ] Simple CRUD moved: plans, insurance, holdings, dividends, finance snapshots, manual trades
- [ ] Each moved endpoint has: Server Action implementation, input validation, error handling
- [ ] Server Actions use user-scoped SSR clients (not service-role)
- [ ] RLS integration tests for each moved endpoint
- [ ] Contract/parity tests proving Server Action returns same data as deprecated FastAPI route
- [ ] Deprecated FastAPI routes documented with rollback toggle
- [ ] `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` usage reduced — only compute/integration endpoints remain on FastAPI

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.4 — ~20 CRUD endpoints move, ~10 compute stay
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 3 — CRUD migration steps
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 3 — contract/parity test requirements
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/05-backend-strategy.md` — endpoint inventory

## Dependencies
- TJ-015 (Supabase SSR client helpers)
- TJ-016 (auth must work for user-scoped clients)
- TJ-007 (RLS policies must be in place)

## Out of Scope
- File uploads, broker sync, backtests, options analytics, market-data jobs (stay in FastAPI)
- Job-trigger Server Actions (TJ-020)
- Removing FastAPI entirely

issues.title: Connect GitHub repo to Vercel and configure environment variables
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Connect the GitHub repository to Vercel, configure the project root at `apps/frontend`, set all environment variables, and verify that push-to-main triggers auto-deploy and PRs get preview deploys.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Vercel project created with root directory set to `apps/frontend`
- [ ] Build settings: Framework=Next.js, Build=`npm run build`, Install=`npm ci`
- [ ] Production env vars set: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (production project)
- [ ] Preview env vars set with dev/preview Supabase credentials (NOT production)
- [ ] `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` set as server-only (no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix)
- [ ] Push to main triggers successful production deploy
- [ ] PR creates preview deploy with working URL
- [ ] Preview deploy uses preview Supabase project (verified by checking project ref in page/API)

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.1 (Frontend) — Vercel project setup
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/02-frontend-strategy.md` — build settings and env vars
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 2 step 4 — connect GitHub to Vercel

## Dependencies
- TJ-004 (Supabase project credentials)
- TJ-008 (CI/CD scaffolding for integration)

## Out of Scope
- Custom domain configuration (deferred per design.md §17)
- Production DNS (TJ-027)
- Vercel Pro plan evaluation

issues.title: Dashboard reads cooked tables with staleness indicators
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Update the main dashboard to read from cooked tables via Server Actions and display `refreshed_at` metadata. Show visual indicators for fresh, refreshing, stale, and failed data states. Add job-trigger Server Actions that enqueue `compute_runs` rows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard reads `cooked_pnl_summary`, `cooked_position_snapshot` via Server Actions
- [ ] `refreshed_at` timestamp displayed on dashboard sections
- [ ] Visual indicators for: fresh (green), refreshing (spinner), stale (amber warning), failed (red banner)
- [ ] "Refresh" button triggers job-trigger Server Action → inserts `compute_runs` row with `status='queued'`
- [ ] Stale threshold configurable (default: data older than 24 hours)
- [ ] Failed state shows last successful refresh time and error summary
- [ ] Playwright test for each visual state

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §6 — household_refresh_state table
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §14 — observability for cooked-refresh failures
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 4 step 3 — stale-data indicators

## Dependencies
- TJ-018 (Server Actions for CRUD must exist)
- TJ-011 (compute worker must exist to process triggered jobs)

## Out of Scope
- Owner email alerts for failures (TJ-029)
- Chart performance optimization
- Real-time WebSocket updates

issues.title: Build household invite flow: send, accept, and revoke invites
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Implement the complete household invite flow: owner sends invite from Settings, signed link generated, spouse clicks link and signs in, accept endpoint verifies and creates membership. Include revocation for pending invites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Settings → Household sharing page where owner enters email + role (`member` or `viewer`)
- [ ] Invite creates `household_invites` row with: normalized citext email, hashed single-use token, 7-day expiry, audit fields
- [ ] `/accept-invite` route: verifies token hash, expiry, email match, household not deleted, not already a member, role not owner
- [ ] On accept: inserts `household_members` row and marks invite accepted — in one transaction
- [ ] Owner can view and revoke pending invites
- [ ] Responses never reveal whether an email has an existing account
- [ ] Rate limiting on invite creation and acceptance
- [ ] Duplicate/reciprocal invite handling per design.md §5

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (Invite flow) — full 6-step flow with DDL
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §13 (Edge Cases) — simultaneous mutual invites, expired link
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/03-auth-sharing-security.md` — invite security requirements

## Dependencies
- TJ-016 (auth must work for sign-in during accept)
- TJ-007 (RLS and household tables must exist)

## Out of Scope
- Supabase magic link integration (use custom signed link initially)
- Email delivery service selection (log invite URL for now; email integration is a follow-up)
- MFA on invite acceptance

issues.title: Implement sharing-specific RLS policies and role enforcement
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Refine and harden RLS policies for the multi-user sharing scenario. Ensure role enforcement (owner/member/viewer), household lifecycle rules, and the edge cases from design.md §13 are covered.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Viewer role: can read household data, cannot write/update/delete
- [ ] Member role: can read and write, cannot manage household (invite/remove/role-change)
- [ ] Owner role: full CRUD + household management; at least one active owner must remain
- [ ] Removed member (`left_at IS NOT NULL`): immediately loses all access
- [ ] Soft-deleted household: no access for any role
- [ ] Concurrent household edits use optimistic concurrency (`updated_at`/version)
- [ ] Spouse-removed-mid-write: Server Actions re-check membership at commit
- [ ] Owner-only endpoints: invite revocation, role changes, member removal, restore
- [ ] Role changes/removals run in transaction with re-check of acting user's active owner role
- [ ] RLS test suite extended for all sharing scenarios

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (Household lifecycle and audit)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §13 (Edge Case Catalogue)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §15 (Acceptance Criteria) #1 — RLS test requirements

## Dependencies
- TJ-007 (base RLS policies)
- TJ-021 (invite flow creates the membership records to test)

## Out of Scope
- Audit logging infrastructure (separate concern)
- Divorce/breakup data export workflow (documented but not implemented in this phase)
- Service-role audit logging

issues.title: Playwright E2E tests for auth, invite, and sharing flows
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create comprehensive Playwright E2E test suite covering the full auth and sharing lifecycle: sign-in, invite, accept, role enforcement, sign-out, and edge cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Signin redirect test: unauthenticated user redirected to `/signin`
- [ ] Google OAuth callback test (mocked or real depending on CI capabilities)
- [ ] Session refresh test: expired session triggers re-auth
- [ ] Invite accept test: spouse receives link, signs in, sees shared data
- [ ] Expired invite test: acceptance fails with generic error
- [ ] Wrong-account invite test: different email cannot accept
- [ ] Sign-out test: session cleared, cache cleared, protected routes denied
- [ ] Role enforcement test: viewer cannot write, member can write, owner can manage
- [ ] Browser-cache regression: private pages not served from cache after sign-out
- [ ] Preview callback spike test against real Supabase + Google (if CI supports)

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 2 — Playwright test requirements
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §13 (Edge Cases) — expired link, wrong-account
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §15 (Acceptance Criteria) #4

## Dependencies
- TJ-016 (auth flow must be implemented)
- TJ-021 (invite flow must be implemented)
- TJ-022 (role enforcement must be implemented)

## Out of Scope
- Performance testing
- Load testing
- Visual regression testing

issues.title: Implement audit trail for household lifecycle events
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Create the audit table that records household lifecycle events: invites, accepts, revokes, role changes, removals/leaves, imports, soft/hard deletes, restores, and privileged worker/service-role actions. Financial data and tokens must be redacted from logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `household_audit_log` table created with: `id`, `household_id`, `user_id`, `action` (enum), `target_user_id`, `metadata` (JSONB), `created_at`
- [ ] Triggers or application-level logging for: invite_created, invite_accepted, invite_revoked, role_changed, member_removed, member_left, household_deleted, household_restored
- [ ] Financial data redacted from audit metadata (no dollar amounts, account numbers)
- [ ] Token hashes redacted (never log raw invite tokens)
- [ ] Service-role actions logged with `actor='service-role'` and request context
- [ ] Audit log readable by household owners only (RLS policy)

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §5 (Household lifecycle and audit)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §16 Risk #9 — financial data in logs

## Dependencies
- TJ-022 (role enforcement and lifecycle management must be in place)

## Out of Scope
- Audit log UI (future work)
- Compliance reporting
- External audit export

issues.title: Validate preview deploys end-to-end with auth and data isolation
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Comprehensively validate that Vercel preview deploys work end-to-end: auth flow, data isolation (preview never hits production Supabase), and basic CRUD. This is the pre-production gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preview deploy OAuth callback working (Google sign-in completes successfully)
- [ ] Preview deploy connects to preview/dev Supabase project (verified by checking project ref)
- [ ] CI assertion passes: preview Supabase ref ≠ production Supabase ref
- [ ] CRUD operations on preview do not affect production data
- [ ] Dashboard loads with cooked data from preview Supabase
- [ ] Household sharing flow works on preview
- [ ] No production secrets accessible from preview environment

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 2 step 5 — verify Vercel preview and production deploys
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §13 (Edge Cases) — preview deploys hitting prod data
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §16 Risk #4 — preview/production data isolation

## Dependencies
- TJ-019 (Vercel connected and configured)
- TJ-016 (auth flow implemented)
- TJ-018 (Server Actions implemented)

## Out of Scope
- Load testing preview deploys
- Stable redirect proxy for preview OAuth (can be deferred if per-PR allowlisting works)

issues.title: Production deploy, DNS configuration, and data migration
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Execute the production deployment: push to main, verify Vercel production deploy, configure DNS (if custom domain decided), run production data migration, and verify the live app.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Production Supabase project has final schema via Alembic `upgrade head` using direct URL
- [ ] Production data backfilled (Jony's household, all existing data migrated)
- [ ] Financial totals verified: production data matches approved baselines
- [ ] Vercel production deploy successful and accessible
- [ ] Google OAuth working on production URL
- [ ] RLS enforcing correctly on production (smoke test)
- [ ] DNS configured if custom domain decided (per design.md §17)
- [ ] SSL certificate valid and HTTPS enforced

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 2 step 5 — production deploy
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §15 (Acceptance Criteria) — full list
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §17 — custom domain decision needed from Jony

## Dependencies
- TJ-025 (preview validation must pass first)
- TJ-012 (data backfill script proven on dev/preview)

## Out of Scope
- Vercel Pro plan upgrade (evaluate after monitoring)
- CDN configuration beyond Vercel defaults
- Multi-region deployment

issues.title: Configure worker Docker healthcheck, restart, and retry policies
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Harden the local Docker worker setup for production reliability: add healthchecks, restart policies, exponential DB reconnect/backoff, and idempotent resume for interrupted jobs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Docker `healthcheck` added to worker container definition
- [ ] `restart: unless-stopped` policy configured
- [ ] Exponential backoff for DB reconnection on network loss / Supabase pause
- [ ] Idempotent resume: `queued`/`running` jobs can be safely re-claimed after restart
- [ ] Worker treats connection refusal/timeouts as retryable
- [ ] Partial compute output never published to cooked tables (verified by test)
- [ ] Pytest tests for: job claiming, retry/backoff, partial failure, pause/resume

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 4 step 4 — Docker healthcheck, restart, backoff
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §11 Phase 4 — worker reliability test requirements
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §13 — Supabase pause + local Docker mid-write
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §16 Risk #5 — laptop sleep / network loss

## Dependencies
- TJ-011 (compute worker framework must exist)

## Out of Scope
- Moving workers to Fly Machines / Cloud Run (future escalation per design.md)
- GitHub Actions cron (TJ-029)

## File paths
- `docker-compose.yml` — worker service definition
- Worker retry/backoff code in `apps/backend/`

issues.title: Disable legacy auth, freeze old FastAPI CRUD routes, and update CORS
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Disable the legacy python-jose/passlib auth in production, remove or gate the deprecated FastAPI CRUD routes (now served by Server Actions), and restrict CORS to the production Vercel domain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Legacy JWT minting code removed or feature-flagged off in production
- [ ] Deprecated CRUD routes return 410 Gone or are removed entirely
- [ ] Rollback toggle documented: re-enable FastAPI routes by setting `LEGACY_API_ENABLED=true`
- [ ] CORS restricted from `allow_origins=["*"]` to production Vercel URL + localhost for dev
- [ ] All remaining FastAPI endpoints (compute/integration) require Supabase JWT
- [ ] No duplicate writes possible (Server Actions and FastAPI cannot both write same data)
- [ ] Contract tests verify deprecated routes are no longer called by frontend

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §15 #10 — legacy auth disabled in production
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 Phase 3 step 3 — deprecate FastAPI routes
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §12 Phase 3 — rollback to FastAPI

## Dependencies
- TJ-017 (Supabase JWKS validation in place)
- TJ-018 (Server Actions must be serving the migrated CRUD)

## Out of Scope
- Removing FastAPI entirely (it still serves compute endpoints)
- Removing the FastAPI Docker container

## File paths
- `apps/backend/main.py` — CORS config, router registration
- `apps/backend/app/api/` — route files to deprecate

issues.title: Post-cutover monitoring, nightly cron, and alerting setup
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Set up post-cutover operational infrastructure: nightly GitHub Actions cron for stale-data checks and database maintenance, owner email alerts after repeated compute failures, and Vercel Analytics monitoring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub Actions nightly cron workflow: checks for `failed` compute_runs, stale domains, writes Actions summary
- [ ] After 2 consecutive failures for a household/domain: email alert to household owner
- [ ] Vercel Analytics enabled and verified
- [ ] Supabase Logs UI accessible and showing query patterns
- [ ] Weekly Vercel egress monitoring documented (alert if approaching 80 GB/month)
- [ ] Encrypted `pg_dump` cron running nightly (from TJ-009 script)
- [ ] Dashboard failure banner shows last successful refresh time per design.md §14

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §4.6 (Observability) — Phase 1 free observability
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §14 (Observability for Cooked-Refresh Failures)
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §8 — monitor Vercel egress weekly
- See `docs/design-hosting/sections/04-deployment-cicd.md` — nightly cron

## Dependencies
- TJ-026 (production must be deployed)
- TJ-027 (worker must be hardened)

## Out of Scope
- Better Stack / Grafana Cloud (Phase 2 observability, only if justified)
- OpenTelemetry tracing (existing instrumentation continues to work)
- Custom alerting dashboard

issues.title: Post-cutover review and decommission local stack
issues.body_markdown:
## Goal

Conduct the final post-cutover review: verify all acceptance criteria from design.md §15 are met, document lessons learned, and freeze/decommission the old local-only stack. This is the epic completion gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 acceptance criteria from design.md §15 verified and documented
- [ ] Production app accessible and functional for Jony (primary user)
- [ ] Household sharing tested with at least one invited user (spouse)
- [ ] Rollback procedures verified: each phase rollback documented and tested
- [ ] Local Docker Postgres no longer the primary database (backup only)
- [ ] Old local auth (python-jose/passlib) confirmed disabled in production
- [ ] Post-cutover monitoring running for at least 1 week with no critical failures
- [ ] Lessons learned documented in `docs/design-hosting/post-cutover-review.md`
- [ ] Epic closed: all TJ-xxx issues in `stage:done`

## References
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §15 (Acceptance Criteria for "Migration Complete")
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §12 (Rollback Rehearsal) — all phases
- See `docs/design-hosting/design.md` §9 (Migration Plan) — full phase overview

## Dependencies
- All Phase 5 issues: TJ-025, TJ-026, TJ-027, TJ-028, TJ-029

## Out of Scope
- Future feature work (IBKR integration, PDF uploads, etc.)
- Supabase Pro upgrade evaluation (tracked separately)
- Fly.io backend hosting evaluation (future escalation path per design.md §7)
